market_briefing.summary: Global markets on March 20, 2026, displayed significant volatility, influenced by Middle East conflict, energy prices, and evolving central bank interest rate expectations. Indian indices rebounded strongly, while US markets remained cautious.
highlights: Indian benchmark indices (Nifty50, Sensex) recovered strongly, with Nifty50 opening above 23,200.
Commodity markets saw extreme volatility; gold edged up but faces weekly pressure, while oil prices cooled to $106 after earlier surges.
Global central banks turned hawkish, with traders scaling back expectations for 2026 rate cuts.
The US SEC formally approved Nasdaq's pilot program for tokenized stock trading.
Natural gas prices remain elevated due to concerns over LNG supply disruptions from the Middle East.
